summary: HiveMind backend + frontend tested end-to-end. Fixed 4 critical ObjectId serialization bugs in server.py (POST /api/requests, /api/requests/{id}/solutions, /api/rewards, /api/rewards/redeem were returning 500 because Motor's insert_one mutated dict to include MongoDB _id). After fix, all 24 backend pytest tests pass (auth, multi-tenant isolation, full lifecycle approve/reject/request_changes, rewards redemption, leaderboard, dashboard, RBAC, audit, notifications, files). Frontend admin login + dashboard + board + create-request + leaderboard + admin tabs work. Found 1 frontend bug: /app/profile/me route triggers runtime error overlay because GET /api/users/me 404s.

ui_bugs.issue: Navigating to /app/profile/me throws Uncaught runtime error overlay with two AxiosError 404s. Frontend Profile.js appears to call GET /api/users/me which does not exist on backend (only PATCH /api/users/me exists; GET requires a real user_id). edit-profile-btn never renders.

integration_issues.issue: Clicking the first redeem-{id} button (highest-credit reward) did not open the confirm-redeem dialog (possibly because the user has insufficient balance and button is disabled, or dialog only opens for affordable rewards). Backend /api/rewards/redeem itself works correctly. Please verify the redeem button is enabled/disabled correctly and shows a tooltip when user lacks credits.

issues: Recharts emits multiple width/height(-1) warnings on initial render — non-blocking but indicates ResponsiveContainer initial sizing issue.

action_items: Fix /app/profile/me crash: either add a backend GET /api/users/me endpoint OR make the Profile page resolve 'me' to the current user's id from AuthContext before calling /api/users/{id}.
Verify redeem button affordability handling on Rewards page — ensure disabled state + tooltip when user cannot afford.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1165 lines — single-file is OK for MVP but consider splitting into routers/models/services if more features added.

Recurring MongoDB _id leak pattern: any endpoint that returns the `doc` dict after `db.X.insert_one(doc)` is vulnerable. Fixed for requests/solutions/rewards/redemptions. Recommend a helper like `def strip_id(d): d.pop('_id', None); return d` or use `{**doc}` only after popping _id.
GET /api/users/{user_id} treats 'me' as a literal id — add a /users/me alias for symmetry with PATCH /users/me.

init_storage() runs sync requests inside FastAPI startup — fine for boot but `requests` is used inside async route handlers (put_object/get_object) which blocks event loop. Consider httpx async client.
CORS allow_origin_regex='.*' with allow_credentials=True is permissive — acceptable for preview but tighten in production.
Reputation thresholds for 'connector' badge use the same metric as 'problem_solver' (line 339 comment says 'simple proxy'). Differentiate when intros category is supported.
test_credentials: admin@acme.com / Admin@123 ; priya@acme.com / Priya@123 ; arjun@acme.com / Arjun@123 ; manager@acme.com / Manager@123 ; reviewer@acme.com / Reviewer@123
seed_data_creation: Tests created several TEST_ prefixed requests and one TEST_ employee + one TEST_ org per run inside acme.com and standalone orgs. Cleanup not implemented (test data is benign).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — runs against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. All 24 backend tests pass after ObjectId fix. Remaining open items are frontend: (1) Profile page /app/profile/me crashes with axios 404; (2) Rewards redeem confirm dialog flow needs UI verification for affordability. The fixes E1 needs to make are frontend-only.
rca of the issue: POST /api/requests returned HTTP 500 with message "'ObjectId' object is not iterable" because the handler returned the `doc` dict immediately after `await db.requests_col.insert_one(doc)`. Motor's insert_one mutates the input dict to inject the generated MongoDB ObjectId under key '_id'. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize ObjectId, raising ValueError. Same pattern existed in /requests/{rid}/solutions, /rewards (admin create), and /rewards/redeem (redemption document). Mitigation: pop '_id' from the doc right after insert_one before returning. Verified by re-running the full pytest suite (24/24 passing) and by exercising the full lifecycle (create→claim→submit→approve) via UI which now navigates to the request detail page successfully.
